{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Excessive sag in flexible ductwork between hangers restricting airflow",
    "Unsealed metal-to-flex joints using non-compliant gray cloth duct tape",
    "Lack of mechanical draw bands or UL 181 listed tape/mastic at connections"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove non-compliant gray cloth duct tape from all joints",
    "Seal all metal-to-flex joints with approved mastic or UL 181A/B listed tape",
    "Install mechanical draw bands (tensioning straps) over the inner vinyl core of the flex duct at connections",
    "Re-hang the flexible duct to eliminate sag, ensuring support spacing meets SMACNA/code standards (maximum 5-foot intervals) and sag does not exceed 1/2 inch per foot"
  ],
  "rationale": "The flexible duct installation fails due to improper support causing sag (which restricts airflow and explains the cold room) and the use of unapproved gray cloth duct tape instead of UL 181 listed materials and mechanical fasteners, violating code requirements for airtight duct construction.",
  "references": [
    "IMC Section 603.9 (Joints, seams and connections)",
    "IMC Section 603.18 (Support of ducts)",
    "SMACNA Fibrous Glass Duct Construction Standards / HVAC Duct Construction Standards",
    "UL 181A / UL 181B Standard for Safety for Closure Systems"
  ]
}
